description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and narrowing towards the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, held tightly together by three dark, weathered metal hoops—one near the top, one at the center, and one near the base. The wood appears aged, with visible grain, subtle cracks, and a rough, matte finish, suggesting long-term exposure to the elements. The top surface is closed, made from the same wood as the sides, and is slightly uneven, indicating handcrafting or wear. There are no visible handles, spouts, or markings, giving it a utilitarian and rustic appearance. This type of barrel is commonly found in outdoor urban settings, such as alleys, courtyards, or near storage areas.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with three dark metal hoops, bulging sides, and a closed, rough wooden top. The surface is aged and rustic, with no visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: The barrel's front shows the bulging wooden staves and all three metal hoops, with the wood grain and rough texture clearly visible.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the curvature of the barrel and the weathered appearance of the metal hoops, with subtle differences in wood grain patterns.
description_view_2: The opposite side view is similar to the front, showing the same arrangement of staves and hoops, with minor variations in wood texture.
description_view_3: This rear view again displays the full set of hoops and the closed wooden top, with the wood's aged surface and slight irregularities in the staves.
